concepts.title: The Earth's Core is divided into two layers
concepts.description: The Earth's Core consists of an inner solid core and an outer liquid core.

concepts.title: Temperature and Pressure Gradient
concepts.description: Temperature increases and pressure decreases as we move from the center of the Earth towards the surface.

concepts.title: Composition of the Earth's Mantle
concepts.description: The Earth's Mantle is composed of hot, viscous rocks that are rich in olivine, pyroxene, and garnet minerals.

concepts.title: Mantle Layers
concepts.description: Use the mnemonic 'AST' to remember the three layers of the Earth's Mantle: Asthenosphere, Transition Zone, and Lithospheric Mantle.

concepts.title: Inner Solid Core vs Outer Liquid Core
concepts.description: Students often get confused between the Inner Solid Core and Outer Liquid Core. To avoid this mistake, remember that the Inner Solid Core is made of iron and nickel, while the Outer Liquid Core is made of iron and nickel alloy.

concepts.title: Earth's Interior Layers
concepts.description: The Earth's interior consists of the Crust, Mantle, Outer Core, and Inner Core. Remember the order of these layers to score well in exams.

concepts.title: The Earth's Crust is broken into several large plates
concepts.description: The Earth's Crust is broken into several large plates that float on the semi-fluid asthenosphere.

concepts.title: Seismic Waves
concepts.description: Seismic waves are generated by earthquakes and can travel through the Earth's interior, providing valuable information about its structure.
